channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Delta flight returns to Atlanta after possible smoke reported in flightdeck
item.description: A Delta flight returned to Atlanta on Monday after the crew observed a “haze” in the aircraft shortly after takeoff, the airline said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Watch live: Trump hosts Macron at White House
item.description: President Donald Trump hosts French President Emmanuel Macron on Monday, the first visit by a European leader to the White House since Trump returned.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KREX 5

item.title: Judge blocks Trump immigration policy allowing arrests in churches for some religious groups
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A federal judge on Monday blocked immigration agents from conducting enforcement operations in houses of worship for Quakers and a handful of other religious groups. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Coast Guard detains 2 undocumented immigrants off Massachusetts coast
item.description: During a routine boat inspection on Thursday, the Coast Guard learned two of the six crew members were not U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: Melanie DaSilva, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court turns down chance to claw back abortion clinic buffer zones
item.description: The Supreme Court turned down an opportunity to overturn its precedent permitting buffer zones around abortion clinics over the objections of two of the court’s leading conservatives. In two orders issued Monday, the court declined to take up challenges to ordinances in Carbondale, Ill.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Trump posts SpongeBob meme mocking federal workers after email requesting accomplishments
item.description: President Donald Trump posted a SpongeBob meme mocking federal employees in the wake of an email sent to the workforce asking them to document their achievements in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war enters third year
item.description: As the war in Ukraine enters its third year, the United States is ramping up efforts to broker a peace deal, but sharp divisions are emerging over how to approach Russia’s role in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, Fox 5

item.title: Federal workers confront mass confusion as Musk's deadline looms
item.description: Confusion and chaos loom as hundreds of thousands of federal employees begin their workweek facing a deadline from Elon Musk to explain their recent accomplishments or risk losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump announces Dan Bongino as Deputy FBI Director
item.description: Bongino is a former U.S. Secret Service agent that became known for his defense of Trump while previously appearing as a Fox News contributor.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hospital patient breaks 'every bone' in nurse's face during attack: Police
item.description: A Florida man is facing attempted murder charges after allegedly attacking a nurse at a hospital where he was being cared for, essentially breaking every bone in her face.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, NewsNation

item.title: US Coast Guard rescues 3 after boat capsizes off Florida coast: video
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard shared footage from a weekend rescue after three overdue boaters were stranded off the coast of Florida when their boat capsized.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: They crossed the Darien Gap to reach the US. Now, boat-by-boat, migrants are returning
item.description: PUERTO CARTÍ, Panama (AP) — They once braved the jungles of the Darien Gap, trekking days along the perilous migrant passage dividing Colombia and Panama with a simple goal: seek asylum in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATIAS DELACROIX and JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pope Francis remains in critical condition with early kidney failure
item.description: Pope Francis was conscious but still receiving high flows of supplemental oxygen Sunday following a respiratory crisis and blood transfusions, as he remains in critical condition with a complex lung infection, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Key federal agencies refuse to comply with Musk's latest demand in his cost-cutting crusade
item.description: Key federal agencies such as the FBI, State Department and Pentagon have instructed their employees not to comply with Elon Musk’s latest demand that they explain what they accomplished last week or risk losing their job.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Beware of text messages about unpaid tolls, FBI and state authorities warn
item.description: Drivers should be on the lookout for scam text messages warning of an unpaid road toll bill, according to recent warnings from authorities.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lynne Marie Stewart of 'Pee-wee's Playhouse,' 'It's Always Sunny' dies: reports
item.description: The actress has recently finished filming for another season of "It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia" and her forthcoming pickleball-related film, "Dink.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Trump, Musk aren't the first to make deep cuts: Clinton-era initiative saved billions
item.description: DOGE isn't the first controversial cost-cutting push — the biggest effort to overhaul the federal government in modern history actually was 30 years ago under a Democratic administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Pope Francis remains in critical condition with early kidney failure
item.description: Pope Francis was conscious but still receiving high flows of supplemental oxygen Sunday following a respiratory crisis and blood transfusions, as he remains in critical condition with a complex lung infection, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk gives all federal workers 48 hours to explain what they did last week or face consequences
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of federal workers have been given little more than 48 hours to explain what they accomplished over the last week, sparking confusion across key agencies as billionaire Elon Musk expands his drive to slash the size of federal government.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Connecticut cannibal killer given conditional release after 2011 murder in which he ate victim's brain, eye
item.description: An institutionalized man who confessed to the 2011 killing of a homeless man in Connecticut in which he also ate the victim's body parts was granted a conditional release.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hawaii man freed after 30 years in prison visits mother's grave and ponders ubiquitous cellphones
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — One of the first places Gordon Cordeiro visited when a judge ordered him released after spending 30 years in prison for a killing he always maintained he had nothing to do with was his mother’s Hawaii gravesite.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump-Putin summit preparations are underway, Russia says
item.description: Preparations are underway for a face-to-face meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin, Russia's deputy foreign minister said Saturday. It marked a clear departure from Western efforts to isolate Moscow over its war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Venezuelan family in US under curtailed humanitarian protections clings to faith amid uncertainty
item.description: HOPKINS, Minn. (AP) — Every Sunday, Johann Teran goes to worship at a Lutheran service in suburban Minneapolis, trying to find some hope that the future he was building isn’t all slipping away.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Briscoe tries to move past devastating penalties and Blaney to start on pole in NASCAR Atlanta race
item.description: A massive fine left Chase Briscoe facing a huge points deficit heading into Sunday's NASCAR Cup Series race at Atlanta Motor Speedway.
item.dc:creator: Charles Odum, Fox 5

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is in critical condition
item.description: The 88-year-old Francis, who has been hospitalized for a week with a complex lung infection, also received blood transfusions after tests showed a condition associated with anemia, the Vatican said in a late update.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Fox 5

item.title: Suspected Tren de Aragua member released on probation in domestic battery case despite 2022 deportation order
item.description: 33 year-old Venezuelan illegal immigrant Pedro Colmenares has been charged with domestic battery, and his deportation was ordered back in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump DEI executive orders
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked parts of President Trump’s executive orders that sought to end the federal government’s backing of diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) efforts.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Chaotic scene unfolded as 3 were shot and killed outside Kentucky driver's license office
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Witnesses described a chaotic scene in which three people were shot and killed outside of a Kentucky driver's license office, including one man shot multiple times by masked assailants.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: You're going to see more 'city-killer' asteroids, but fret not: MIT astronomer
item.description: This week's hype around a "city-killing" asteroid possibly striking Earth raised the fears of many before the odds of impact dropped to nearly nothing, but the MIT scientist who developed the risk scale for this type of event said it still has a lot to teach the public.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, WGN 9
